2603.14462.title: STAG-CN: Spatio-Temporal Apiary Graph Convolutional Network for Disease Onset Prediction in Beehive Sensor Networks
2603.14462.paper_summary: Honey bee colony losses threaten global pollination services, yet current monitoring systems treat each hive as an isolated unit, ignoring the spatial pathways through which diseases spread across apiaries. This paper introduces the Spatio-Temporal Apiary Graph Convolutional Network (STAG-CN), a graph neural network that models inter-hive relationships for disease onset prediction. STAG-CN operates on a dual adjacency graph combining physical co-location and climatic sensor correlation among hive sessions, and processes multivariate IoT sensor streams through a temporal--spatial--temporal sandwich architecture built on causal dilated convolutions and Chebyshev spectral graph convolutions. Evaluated on the Korean AI Hub apiculture dataset (dataset \#71488) with expanding-window temporal cross-validation, STAG-CN achieves an F1 score of 0.607 at a three-day forecast horizon. An ablation study reveals that the climatic adjacency matrix alone matches full-model performance (F1\,=\,0.607), while the physical adjacency alone yields F1\,=\,0.274, indicating that shared environmental response patterns carry stronger predictive signal than spatial proximity for disease onset. These results establish a proof-of-concept for graph-based biosecurity monitoring in precision apiculture, demonstrating that inter-hive sensor correlations encode disease-relevant information invisible to single-hive approaches.
2603.14462.paper_summary_zh: 蜜蜂群落的損失威脅全球的授粉服務，然而目前的監測系統將每個蜂箱視為孤立單位，忽略了疾病在養蜂場之間傳播的空間途徑。本文介紹了時空養蜂圖卷積網絡（STAG-CN），這是一種圖神經網絡，用於建模蜂箱之間的關係以預測疾病的發生。STAG-CN在一個雙重鄰接圖上運作，結合了蜂箱會議之間的物理共址和氣候傳感器相關性，並通過基於因果膨脹卷積和切比雪夫光譜圖卷積構建的時間--空間--時間三明治架構處理多變量物聯網傳感器流。STAG-CN在韓國AI Hub養蜂數據集（數據集\#71488）上進行了擴展窗口的時間交叉驗證，達到了三天預測範圍內的F1分數0.607。消融研究顯示，僅使用氣候鄰接矩陣的性能與完整模型相匹配（F1\,=\,0.607），而僅使用物理鄰接則產生F1\,=\,0.274，這表明共享的環境反應模式對於疾病的發生提供了比空間接近性更強的預測信號。這些結果為精準養蜂中的基於圖的生物安全監測建立了概念驗證，證明了蜂箱之間的傳感器相關性編碼了對單一蜂箱方法不可見的與疾病相關的信息。
2603.14462.author: Sungwoo Kang et.al.
